results.summary:
This file is clean of owner-vocabulary migration debt. files-pane.vue is a sidebar tree-panel (header + batch-ops toolbar + upload-status strip + a ContextMenu + a DropdownMenu + five small Dialogs), not a settings surface, and it contains zero SettingsSection/SettingsRow/FieldStack/MetricReadout/ExpandableSettingsRow/PersonaTile/CalloutBanner/BackendCard usage or hand-rolled equivalents. Walking all five click-to-open dialogs (New File, New Folder, Rename, Delete confirm, Batch Delete confirm) found the same repeated shape each time: a bare placeholder-only Input (no Label, no FieldStack wrapper) or a plain muted confirmation

, plus the stock DialogFooter Cancel/Confirm pair. This could look at first glance like an unmigrated FieldStack instance, but cross-referencing two other already-refactored, non-settings dialogs in the same product family -- sidebar/recents.vue's rename-session dialog and bots/components/avatar-edit-dialog.vue -- shows this bare-Input-no-Label pattern is the established, deliberate convention for a minimal single-field 'name this thing' dialog, distinct from a settings-page multi-field form (which does use FieldStack, per schedule-editor.vue). So these are classified DIFF-RELATIONSHIP, not MISS. The pane header and batch-ops bar are sidebar-specific chrome with no owner-shape analog (confirmed by the file's own comment documenting intentional cross-pane header consistency with Chat/Schedule). The ContextMenu and DropdownMenu are both textbook-compliant compositions of the real @memohai/ui atoms. Net result: 0 MISS findings -- this file was correctly out of scope for the Phase 3 batch migration because it never contained a settings-owner shape to begin with.

findings.why: Looks like it could be a FieldStack/FormStack occurrence (a form with space-y-4 inside a Dialog, matching the New Task dialog's outer anatomy: space-y-4 column + DialogFooter with ghost Cancel + filled primary).
